lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: Run! Run for your life! Save yourself! I'll keep them back as long as I can...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do I need to run?

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: Your very life is in danger. Rashiliyia has returned and we are all doomed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Rashiliyia? Who is she?

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: Rashiliyia is the Queen of the dead. She has returned and has brought a plague of undead with her. They now occupy our village and we have them trapped. I warn people like yourself to stay away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can we do?

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: We're doing all we can to keep the undead at bay. The village is covered in a deadly green mist. If you go into the village, a terrible sickness will befall you.

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: And the undead are even stronger beyond the gates. My guess is that it has something to do with the Legend of Rashiliyia.

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: But you would need to talk to the Shaman in Tai Bwo Wannai village to get more details about that. I really have to go now and fight these undead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are the undead here?

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: Rashiliyia! The Queen of the undead has risen! She is the mother of the undead creatures that roam this land. But alas I know nothing of the legend that surrounds her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Legend you say?

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: Yes.... I said it is a legend that I know nothing about.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, Ok, sorry for bothering you.

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: Ok, perhaps you'd like to be on your way now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh come on, you must know something?

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: Well, let me think now...

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: Hmmm, there was something I think that might help...

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: Nope, sorry. It's gone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe you know someone who does know something?

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: My guess is that this has something to do with the legend of Rashiliyia. But you need to speak to the Shaman in 'Tai Bwo Wannai' village to get more details about that.

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: I really have to fight these undead now Bwana, before they take over the world!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go to see the Shaman.

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: Well, that would be helpful Bwana. If you're sure you want to go, you can take a Wampum belt to him for me. It will give the Shaman, Trufitus the story of our problems down here. Are you sure you want to go?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Errr, I'm having second thoughts now.

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: That's Ok Bwana, it's a big responsibility. Come back and see me if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'm sure and I'll take the Wampum belt to Trufitus.

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: I would be very grateful if you did. Here take this...

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: Please can you give it to Trufitus. He may be able to give you extra details on the legend surrounding Rashiliyia. Good luck!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok. Thanks for your help.

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: You're welcome Bwana, good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, but I don't think this is something that I can help with at the moment!

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: Ok, I understand Bwana! You may as well be on your way then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I help in any way?

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: I don't think so Bwana, but you may want to consult with Trufitus the Shaman in Tai Bwo Wannai village, he may have some information which could help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh, it sounds nasty, just the kind of thing I want to avoid!

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: Quite right, Bwana, please make all haste! Before your spine turns to water as we speak.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What danger is there around here?

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: Can you not see Bwana? This whole area is infested with the living dead.

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: Arrggghhh, here are some now!

lines.speaker: Undead one
lines.text: Rahhhhh! OOoohhhhhh!

lines.speaker: Undead one
lines.text: Prepare to go to the other side!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah... Ok, I'm running!

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: God speed to you my friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: I am Mosol Rei, I am a Jungle Warrior. I used to live in this village, but it is too dangerous for you to stay around here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mosol Rei, that's a nice name.

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: Thanks, but you really should leave.

sections.title: Talking to Mosol Rei after getting the Wampum belt

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are the undead here?

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: Rashiliyia! The Queen of the undead has risen! She is the mother of the undead creatures that roam this land. But alas I know nothing of the legend that surrounds her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Legend you say?

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: Yes.... I said it is a legend that I know nothing about.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe you know someone who does know something?

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: My guess is that this has something to do with the legend of Rashiliyia. But you need to speak to the Shaman in 'Tai Bwo Wannai' village to get more details about that.

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: I really have to fight these undead now Bwana, before they take over the world!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go to see the Shaman.

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: Well, if you go to see the Shaman, please give him that Wampum belt I gave to you, it will give him more details about our situation down here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'm sure and I'll take the Wampum belt to Trufitus.

lines.speaker: Mosol Rei
lines.text: That's great Bwana, if you give Trufitus the Wampum belt I gave you, it will give him more details of our current situation.

sections.title: Using the Wampum belt on Trufitus

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Hello Bwana, this message from Mosol Rei bears bad news... Yes, things do look very bad indeed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know about Rashiliyia?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Hmmm, it's been a long time since I heard that name. Rashiliyia is the Queen of the Undead. And a more fearsome enemy you will be unlikely to find.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: I fear that you bring me news that she has returned to plague us once again? Alas I know of no weakness that she has.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So there is nothing we can do?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Not that I can think of.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, ok!

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Yes, it's a bit sad really, I liked that village.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Well, I hope you will excuse me, but I need to get back to my studies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Should I start to evacuate the island?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Yes, that may be a good idea. Many people could die! If only there was a way to defeat her!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mosol Rei said something about a legend?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Ah, yes, there is a legend, but it is lost in the midst of antiquity...

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: The last place to hold any details regarding this mystery was in the temple of Ah Za Rhoon....and that has long since vanished... it crumbled into dust...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why was it called Ah Za Rhoon?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: It is from an ancient language. The direct translation is... 'Magnificence floating on water'. But my research makes me believe that the temple was built on land.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: And most likely between large bodies of water, for example large lakes. However, many people have searched for the temple, and have failed. I would hate to see you waste your time on a pointless search like that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the information!

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: What information?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About Ah Za Rhoon and where it is.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Hmmm, well, you are welcome bwana.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know anything more about the temple?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Not much... I would say that is about it...

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Even the great priest Zadimus who built the temple did not survive. Some say that Rashiliyia caused the temple to collapse.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: She was angry at Zadimus for not returning her affections. She was a great sorceress even before they met.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: I don't know anymore. You're very demanding aren't you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am going to search for Ah Za Rhoon!

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: What?! You must be crazy! That place has passed into myth and legend, it has been buried under rubble for years. It's most likely buried 20 men deep, and that's if you can actually find it.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Are you sure you're going to go and look for it? I may be able to do some research into this if you agree. Only I don't want to waste my time if you're not serious about this!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I will seriously look for Ah Za Rhoon and I'd appreciate your help.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Ok then Bwana, good luck with your quest, and remember to stock up well with adventuring supplies before setting off. You never know how useful some fairly ordinary things might be when you're

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: adventuring.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: I'll hold on to this Wampum belt for you for the time being. I'll give it back to you when we have completed this quest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, now it comes to it, I'm having second thoughts.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Well, I understand. Perhaps you can search for it another time? Come back when you think you're ready. Excuse me now won't you while I return to my studies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a pity that I can't search for Ah Za Rhoon now.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Well, I understand. Perhaps you can search for it another time? Come back when you think you're ready. Excuse me now won't you while I return to my studies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are there any traps there?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: How am I supposed to know? A lot of what I know is most probably wrong but some of it seems right to me. Excuse me but I must get back to my studies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will you pack your things now?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: I will wait and see what will happen. Maybe Rashiliyia does not have the power to strike too far from her resting place? But there are many things that I need to do now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is her resting place important?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: I believe it is! It might be that her physical remains are the focal point of her supernatural powers. It is said that many years ago, a group of adventurers once infiltrated her tomb to try to rid the world of Rashiliyia.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: These adventurers reported seeing a wraith- like creature.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Although the adventurers disturbed Rashiliyia's bones, they were not able to properly sanctify them. And this is the most likely reason why she still plagues us today.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Of course, she only has to order one of her minions to move her bones and she can quite quickly and easily set up a new headquarters anywhere and continue to launch her plague of undead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are minions?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Minions are the fiendish undead creatures that she controls. She has very few living worshippers, but they need to be dealt with at some point.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does she have any weaknesses?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: I am not sure, but the legend about her certainly is long. It's a pity that the temple of Ah Za Rhoon has crumbled as there may be some clues that could help us to defeat her.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: I think the largest problem will be in locating her resting place.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are onions?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Surely you mean Minions?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes of course, I mean Minions, what made you think I said Onions?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Minions are the fiendish undead creatures that Rashiliyia controls. She has very few living worshippers, but they need to be dealt with at some point.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Usually a strong creature of some sort will be guarding the bones and it is not to be tackled lightly.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Hello Bwana, how goes your quest to find Ah Za Rhoon?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well thanks...

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Well that's very good news. Let me know if you find anything useful, I may be able to help out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm, I don't know where to look?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Hmm, that doesn't surprise me. The only information I have refers to its name. Ah Za Rhoon, its name means 'Magnificence Floating on Water'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think it was floating on water?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: It's very doubtful. I suspect it was built to 'appear' as if it was floating on water. Perhaps on an island or between large bodies of water.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: If you search for somewhere like this, you may find something worth investigating.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok thanks for your help.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: You're welcome Bwana, I only hope it helped.

sections.title: Use any unrelated item on Trufitus

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: I'm sorry Bwana but I just don't have a use for that!

sections.title: Use spade on Mound of earth

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It looks very dangerous, and dark... Scary!

sections.title: Use lit black candle on Fissure
Look at Fissure after rope is attached
Search Fissure after rope is attached

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It looks very dangerous, and dark... Scary!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ** Gulp! **

sections.title: Use chisel on Strange looking stone

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oops! Not enough wood left to do anything else with the table!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Weeeeeeee!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Weeeeeeee!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: * Uh oh! *

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oops! Not enough wood left to do anything else with the table!

sections.title: Craft Smashed table after using up the wood
Craft Smashed table after making the raft

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arrgghhhh!

sections.title: Talking to Trufitus after visiting Ah Za Rhoon

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Greetings...

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Greetings Bwana, you have been away! The situation with Rashiliyia is worsening! I pray that you have some good news for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I found the temple of Ah Za Rhoon.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Well that sounds great Bwana. Tell me, what did you find?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need help with Bervirius.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Bervirius is the son of Rashiliyia. His tomb may hold some clues as to how Rashiliyia may be defeated.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some items that I need help with.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Well, just let me see the item and I'll help as much as I can.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: We need to identify that the place you have found is indeed Ah Za Rhoon.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Any scrolls or information about Rashiliyia's kin would be helpful.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Have you got any items concerning Rashiliyia? If so, please show me them.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: There must be something relating to Zadimus at the temple. Did you find anything? If so, let me see it.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: And best of luck!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need help with Zadimus.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Zadimus is a spirit yearning for freedom. Bury him in a sacred place to release his spirit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there any sacred ground around here?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: The ground in the centre of the village is very sacred to us. Maybe you could try there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks!

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: You're quite welcome Bwana.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need help with Rashiliyia.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: We need to find Rashiliyia's resting place and learn how to put her spirit to rest. You may find some clues to her resting place in Ah Za Rhoon or Bervirius' Tomb.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some help with the Temple of Ah Za Rhoon.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: If you have found the temple, you should search it thoroughly and see if there are any clues about Rashiliyia.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: We need to identify that the place you have found is indeed Ah Za Rhoon.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Any scrolls or information about Rashiliyia's kin would be helpful.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Have you got any items concerning Rashiliyia? If so, please show me them.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: There must be something relating to Zadimus at the temple. Did you find anything? If so, let me see it.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: And best of luck!

sections.title: Using the Zadimus corpse on Trufitus

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you make of this?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: ! GASP ! That's incredible, where did you find it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found the corpse in a decomposing gallows. I get a very strange feeling every time I try to bury the body.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Hmmm, that sounds very strange. I sense a spirit in torment, you should try to bury the remains.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there any sacred ground around here?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: The ground in the centre of the village is very sacred to us. Maybe you could try there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you dispose of this for me?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: I dare not touch it. I am a spiritual man and the spirit of this being may possess me and turn me into a minion of Rashiliyia.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you decipher this please?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: This is an ancient artefact!

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: I can certainly try! Hmm, incredible, it seems very ancient and mentions something about Zadimus and Ah Za Rhoon. It says, 'Here lies the traitor Zadimus, let his spirit be forever tormented'.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: If you have found anything else that you need help with, please just let me know.

sections.title: Using the tattered scroll on Trufitus

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you make of this?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Truly amazing Bwana, this scroll must be ancient. I'm not sure if I get more meaning from it than you though. Perhaps Bervirius' tomb is still accessible?

sections.title: Using the crumpled scroll on Trufitus

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have a look at this, tell me what you think.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: I am speechless Bwana, this is truly ancient. Where did you find it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In an underground building of some sort.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: You must truly have found the temple of Ah Za Rhoon! The scroll gives some interesting details about Rashiliyia, some things I didn't know before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything that can help?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Hmmm, well just that part about the wards...

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: It may be possible to make a ward like that... But what is the best thing to make it from? Perhaps something close to Bervirius, an item of some significance to him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks!

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: You're quite welcome Bwana.

sections.title: Burying the Zadimus corpse in the wrong place
Burying the Zadimus corpse in the right place

lines.speaker: Spirit of Zadimus
lines.text: You have released me from my torture, and now I shall aid you. You seek to dispell the one who tortured and killed me. Remember this... 'I am the key, but only kin may approach her.'

sections.title: Using the bone shard on Trufitus

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you have a look at this please?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: It looks like a simple shard of bone. Why do you think it is significant?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It appeared when I buried Zadimus' corpse.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Ah, interesting, so you think that Zadimus gave you the bone? What makes you say that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He said something after he gave it to me.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: What did he say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The spirit said something about keys and kin?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Hmmm, maybe it's a clue of some kind?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: If you found anything at Ah Za Rhoon that you're not sure of, why not investigate it or show it to me and I'll see what I can make of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The spirit rambled on about some nonsense.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Oh, so it most likely was not very important then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Oh, right. Come back and talk with me if you get an idea.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No reason really.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Well why are you showing it to me then?

sections.title: Talking to Trufitus after burying Zadimus' corpse

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Greetings...

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Greetings Bwana, you have been away! The situation with Rashiliyia is worsening! I pray that you have some good news for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I found the temple of Ah Za Rhoon.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Well that sounds great Bwana. Tell me, what did you find?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have just buried Zadimus' corpse.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Something seems different about you. You look like you have seen a ghost?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It just so happens that I have!

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Oh! So you managed to bury Zadimus's corpse?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, it was pretty grisly!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The spirit said something about keys and kin?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Hmmm, maybe it's a clue of some kind?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Rashiliyia's only kin was a son, 'Bervirius'. His remains were entombed on a small island which lies to the South West. I will do some research into this to see if I can find any other details.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: But I think we must take Zadimus' clue literally and get some item that belonged to Bervirius as it may be the only way to approach Rashiliyia. Perhaps something like this exists in his tomb?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The spirit rambled on about some nonsense.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Oh, so it most likely was not very important then.

sections.title: Use Bronze wire on Bone beads

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arrggghhhhhh!

sections.title: Talking to Trufitus after visiting Bervirius' Tomb

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Greetings...

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Greetings Bwana, did you find Bervirius' Tomb?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I found Bervirius' Tomb.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Congratulations Bwana, show me any items you have found though. I may be able to help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I actually need help with something else.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: What could I possibly help you with Bwana?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I didn't find anything in the tomb.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Maybe you need to look around a little more. There must be some small detail at least that can help us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I didn't find a thing.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: That is a shame Bwana. We really do need to act against Rashiliyia soon if we are ever to stand a chance of defeating her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually I did find the tomb, I was just joking.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Well, Bwana, this is no laughing matter. We need to take this very seriously and act now! If you have found any items at the tomb that you need help with please let me see them and I will help as much as I can.

sections.title: Using the Sword pommel on Trufitus

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: It is a very nice item Bwana. It may be just what you need to gain access to Rashiliyia's tomb. While you were away, I did some research.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Rashiliyia would spare the lives of those who wore bronze necklaces. This pommel may have some significance to Bervirius. Perhaps you can craft something from it that can help?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: My guess is that you will need some protection from Rashiliyia if you intend to enter her tomb!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I make a bronze necklace?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Well, Bwana, I would guess that you would need to get some bronze metal and work it into something that could be turned into a necklace?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I put on the necklace?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Perhaps Zadimus' clue has the answer? Now, what was it that he said again? Something about kin and keys? That sword pommel belonged to Bervirius didn't it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: You're more than welcome Bwana! Good luck for the rest of your quest.

sections.title: Using the Locating crystal on Trufitus

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: This is incredible Bwana.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It is?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Absolutely! This will help you to locate the entrance to Rashiliyia's tomb. Simply activate it when you think you are near, and it should glow different colours to show how near you are.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Hmm, these notes are quite extraordinary Bwana. They give location details of Rashiliyia's tomb, and some information on how to use the crystal.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: The information is quite specific, North of Ah Za Rhoon! That's a great place to start looking!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Take a look at these.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Hmm, very interesting Bwana, your abilities are much more focused than I had initially thought. I presume these are to be part of the ward to protect you from Rashiliyia?

sections.title: Using Beads of the dead on Trufitus

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Take a look at this...

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: This is very impressive Bwana, I'm quite surprised at your ingenuity. This should be a good protection against Rashiliyia if you ever find her Tomb.

sections.title: Using the Bone key on Trufitus

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have a look at this!

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: This is amazing Bwana, the level of detail is incredible. Where did you find it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I made it from the bone shard that Zadimus gave me.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: How very inventive Bwana. You must have seen the lock to have crafted it so well. Does the key work?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes and I explored inside some sort of cavern.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: How interesting Bwana, did you find anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not really.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Maybe you should go back and try to find some more things.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Show me any other items that you may have. We need any clue to locate Rashiliyia's resting place.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I found lots of things.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: If you let me see them Bwana, perhaps I can offer you some extra information.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know, I haven't tried it yet.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: It may be an idea to try it and then scout out the area. If it relates to Rashiliyia, it might help us to defeat her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know what it opens?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: You must already know what it opens to have carved it so pefectly. Perhaps in your travels you have come across some unique doors with a unique lock? I hope this helps with your quest.

sections.title: Use Bone key on Carved doors

lines.speaker: Rashiliyia
lines.text: Let me squeeze the life from your mortal frame.

lines.speaker: Rashiliyia
lines.text: Which non-kin dares enter my tomb?

lines.speaker: Rashiliyia
lines.text: My minions will finish thee!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: * Gaaaa.... *

sections.title: Open Tomb doors before placing bones

lines.speaker: Nazastarool
lines.text: I am Nazastarool! Prepare to die!

lines.speaker: Nazastarool
lines.text: Quake in fear, for I am reborn!

lines.speaker: Nazastarool
lines.text: Your death will be swift.

lines.speaker: Nazastarool
lines.text: Nazastarool returns with vengeance!

lines.speaker: Nazastarool
lines.text: Soon you will serve Rashiliyia.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh no, I'm going to be stuck in here forever!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How will I ever get out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm too young to die!

sections.title: Use bone key on tomb exit
Talking to Trufitus after visiting the Tomb of Rashiliyia

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Greetings again Bwana. I hope that you have managed to locate Rashiliyia's Tomb. Again, if you found anything interesting, please show it to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do now?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Well Bwana, if you have Rashiliyia's remains, you need to find a way to put her spirit to rest. Perhaps there was a clue with one of the artefacts that you have?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Why not have a look through the artefacts that you have found and see if there is some clue that might help? If you do not have her remains, you will need to find them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: You're more than welcome Bwana! Good luck for the rest of your quest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you have a look at this..

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: This is truly incredible bwana... So these are the remains of the dread Queen Rashiliyia?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I think so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do with them?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: Hmm, I'm not exactly sure... Perhaps there is a clue in one of the artefacts you have found?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you take them off my hands?

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: I dare not take them, I may be taken over by the evil spirit of Rashiliyia!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Trufitus
lines.text: You're more than welcome Bwana! Good luck for the rest of your quest.

sections.title: Dropping Rashiliyias' remains on the ground

lines.speaker: Rashiliyia
lines.text: You have my gratitude for releasing me, there is still much that needs to be done before Shilo Village is utterly destroyed. Please excuse me, I have plans and people to execute!

sections.title: Using Rashiliyias' remains on the dolmen

lines.speaker: Rashiliyia
lines.text: You have my gratitude for releasing my spirit. I have suffered a vengeful and evil existence. I was tricked by Zamorak. He returned my son to me as an undead creature.

lines.speaker: Rashiliyia
lines.text: My hatred and bitterness corrupted me. I tried to destroy all life... now I am released. And am grateful to contemplate eternal rest...

sections.title: Look-at or Investigate Strange looking stone
Using items on Strange looking stone
Look-at or Search the Ancient gallows
Using bones on the Tomb door
